test_output_summary: 16/16 tests passed in tests/unit/test_ordering.py; 119/119 tests passed across the full forge.memory unit-test suite (test_ordering, test_writer, test_models, test_redaction, test_supersession). Black --check reports all three modified files unchanged.
implementation_notes: Added forge/memory/ordering.py with a single thin coordinator record_stage_event(persist_to_sqlite, group_id) that (1) invokes the SQLite persistence callable synchronously, (2) only on success calls fire_and_forget_write(entity, group_id), and (3) returns the persisted entity. SQLite exceptions propagate verbatim; Graphiti failures are absorbed by fire_and_forget_write. Re-exported the helper from forge.memory.__init__ so stage hooks import the canonical seam from the package root (closing the loophole where a hook could reach for fire_and_forget_write directly). Followed the implementation note to keep the coordinator thin: no retry logic, no compensation, no extra logging — compensation is reconcile-backfill's job (TASK-IC-004).
concerns: AC-005 verifies SQLite-committed timestamp precedes Graphiti dispatch via a wall-clock spy at the dispatcher entry point (no network-side `created_at` is observable from the helper itself); the BDD step impl for `@edge-case write-ordering` (TASK-IC-011) will exercise the same invariant against the Gherkin scenario.
Black is installed targeting Python 3.14 in the runtime; the formatter prints a safety-check warning under Python 3.12 but still validates the modified files cleanly with --check.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `record_stage_event()` is the single helper every stage hook calls
completion_promises.evidence: Added record_stage_event in src/forge/memory/ordering.py and re-exported it from forge.memory (added to __all__). TestRecordStageEventIsSingleHelper asserts the helper is callable, lives in forge.memory.ordering, is reachable as `from forge.memory import record_stage_event`, and is listed in ordering_module.__all__.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] SQLite commit happens BEFORE Graphiti dispatch (no parallel; no reordering)
completion_promises.evidence: record_stage_event calls persist_to_sqlite() synchronously and only after it returns invokes fire_and_forget_write(entity, group_id). TestRecordStageEventOrdering uses a shared call-order list to prove `sqlite` is appended before `graphiti`, and additionally asserts the entity returned by SQLite is the exact value forwarded to the Graphiti dispatcher.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] If SQLite commit raises, Graphiti dispatch does NOT happen (no orphan Graphiti writes without SQLite anchor)
completion_promises.evidence: The implementation calls fire_and_forget_write only after persist_to_sqlite() returns, so any exception raised by the callable propagates and short-circuits dispatch. TestRecordStageEventSqliteFailure patches fire_and_forget_write and asserts spy.assert_not_called() across three failure shapes (custom subclass of RuntimeError, ValueError, and a sentinel RuntimeError instance), and verifies the original exception is propagated verbatim.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] If Graphiti dispatch fails, SQLite entry is still durable (verified by reconcile-backfill picking it up next build)
completion_promises.evidence: record_stage_event returns the SQLite-persisted entity regardless of Graphiti dispatch outcome — fire_and_forget_write is contractually silent-swallow, so the helper never undoes the SQLite commit. TestRecordStageEventGraphitiFailure exercises the realistic quiet-dispatch path (returns the entity for reconcile-backfill to pick up) and confirms the helper does not block on dispatcher completion. Reconcile-backfill (TASK-IC-004) will detect SQLite-anchored entries missing from Graphiti.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] Covers `@edge-case write-ordering`: assertion that SQLite-committed timestamp precedes Graphiti `created_at`
completion_promises.evidence: TestRecordStageEventTimestampPrecedence captures a wall-clock timestamp inside the patched fire_and_forget_write at dispatch time and asserts the entity's SQLite-side decided_at is strictly less than that captured value. A second test flips a `persist_returned` marker just before the persist callable returns and asserts the dispatcher sees the marker as True — proving dispatch only runs after persist returns. The BDD step impl for `@edge-case write-ordering` is the responsibility of TASK-IC-011 per the task spec.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: Ran `black --check src/forge/memory/ordering.py src/forge/memory/__init__.py tests/unit/test_ordering.py` — all three files report `would be left unchanged`. Both files use 4-space indentation, 88-char max line length, snake_case functions, type hints throughout, and follow the existing module-docstring conventions in src/forge/memory/.
